fer: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Feroge.

fie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fyer.
fin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Finnish.
fine: adjective. Senses referring to subjective quality.
finer: adjective. comparative form of fine: more fine
fino: noun. The driest and palest type of traditional sherry.
fir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Firan.

firer: noun. A person who fires a weapon; a shooter.
firn: noun. A type of old snow which has gone through multiple thaw and refreeze cycles and thus is made of numerous small icy grains, though it
fon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Fon.
for: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fore.
fore: adjective. Former; occurring earlier (in some order); previous.
fro: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Old French.
froe: noun. A cleaving tool for splitting cask staves and shingles from a block of wood.
infer: verb. To introduce (something) as a reasoned conclusion; to conclude by reasoning or deduction, as from premises or evidence.
inferior: adjective. Lower in rank, status, or quality.
inro: noun. A small decorative box, hung from the sash of a kimono, for holding small objects.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
iron: noun. A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel: a
irone: noun. Any of several ketones, or a mixture of such, found in orris oil (oil extracted from iris roots), used as odorants in perfumes.
ironer: noun. Someone who irons, someone who does ironing.
neif: noun. A woman born in the state of serfdom; a female serf.
noir: adjective. Of or pertaining to film noir, or the atmosphere associated with that genre

nori: noun. A type of seaweed, a red alga, laver (genus Pyropia, including species P. yezoensis and P. tenera).
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
